programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explore Oradour-sur-Glane, a French town drenched in infamy, the victim of a notorious raid, Alcatraz, a facility famed for breakouts, subjected to a daring break-in, and Sealand, a micronation off the English coast, stormed by a band of mercenaries.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The Tunnels of Gibraltar were a Second World War espionage base, hidden in the Rock of Gibraltar, which lay undiscovered for decades. At Atlas-1 Test Site, are the remains of an ingenious US Airforce project that continues to keep people safe.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In the Scottish capital of Edinburgh, hidden from view, a series of vaults tell a murky story of opportunity, crime and destitution. programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Officers from Sun Hill are called to deal with an apparently motiveless attack in a pub.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil has to deal with some demons from the past when a woman is assaulted.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil is convinced that someone has a vendetta against Julia Bickham. Jared Miles proves to be innocent and identifies a man he saw with Thomas Sands - Stephen Fairfax. It emerges that Julia gave evidence at the inquest a couple of years before of a young woman suicide which meant that Fairfax lost an insurance claim and the custody of his daughter. Then, he kidnaps Julia.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When the police receive an emergency call informing them that a newsagent called O'Halloran has been beaten to death, Banksy is shocked to learn that the anonymous message came from Joseph Sissoulu's mobile. Prints found at the scene are traced to Mo Campbell - a friend of Joseph's murdered son. Mo eventually admits to the murder, but is he telling the whole story?

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DI Neil Manson and DS Max Carter investigate when 17-year-old Ashley Simmons is found shot dead and dumped in a warehouse. Their enquiries lead them to the children's home where she once lived, but friends and family there all claim that she was a clean-living teen. Evidence points to Ashley's mother's boyfriend, whose car was spotted at the crime scene.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: DS Max Carter and DC Jo Masters question Paul Brewer when a raid discloses indecent images of a young girl on his laptop. While at first denying responsibilty, Paul then points the finger of suspicion at paedophile George Fielding, a man he met in prison, and to whom he had recently lent the computer. When DC Jacob Banks questions Fielding, he immediately verifies Paul's story, but Banks is suspicious of both men's statements.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max and Jo raid the house of Jimmy Mack but fail to find his consignment of drugs - or his missing girlfriend, Rochelle. Sally and Roger visit the home of former prostitute and drug addict Fran Morris, where her young son Noah has collapsed. Then Rochelle is found tied to a chair in Fran's flat, having been beaten up.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for the people inside.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A dangerous driver in a pick-up truck causes carnage when his vehicle rolls over and the HART team are called to free him and his passenger from the wreckage.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A teenage forestry worker receives a life-threatening injury; severe asthma endangers a mum's life; the Police help young divers safely to shore.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A woman is caught on the wrong end of a fishing spear; a daring rescue at sea; a chopper crew welcomes a new life mid-flight; the Police catch a boatie making waves.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When the local council insists that Ben is disabled, what can he do but play along with it until the wheels start coming off?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike, a cash-strapped young couple struggling to buy their first home, think their prayers are answered when a distant relative bequeaths them a sprawling estate. However, the old house is haunted by the ghosts of its former inhabitants.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Still recovering from her near-fatal accident, Alison finds herself hounded by the ghostly gaggle, who now realise that she can both see and hear them. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the old house into a home leads to a DIY disaster.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: On Pat's `death day', he prepares himself for his family's annual pilgrimage to the site of his demise. Realising that Alison's gift makes her a conduit between the dead and the living, he begs her to pass on a message from the other side.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan thinks her Polish help is stealing from her, someone confuses `bing' with `bling', and a young couple has to live in their car so their baby can sleep.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Margaret opens her birthday cards; Elaine Figgis' fiance is a cannibalistic multiple murderer on death row; the Irish nurse is feeling a little romantic.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Working mum Toyah, US-born dad Ron, and their three kids, moved from a big house in the US into a much smaller house in the UK. Unfortunately, they forgot to downsize their possessions and are now stuck in a home that doesn't serve its purpose.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels and a lifetime of photos from her years as a singing waitress, let go of the past and remind her that she's not dead yet.

programme.title: First Responders

programme.title: First Responders

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When the local council insists that Ben is disabled, what can he do but play along with it until the wheels start coming off?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike, a cash-strapped young couple struggling to buy their first home, think their prayers are answered when a distant relative bequeaths them a sprawling estate. However, the old house is haunted by the ghosts of its former inhabitants.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Still recovering from her near-fatal accident, Alison finds herself hounded by the ghostly gaggle, who now realise that she can both see and hear them. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the old house into a home leads to a DIY disaster.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: On Pat's `death day', he prepares himself for his family's annual pilgrimage to the site of his demise. Realising that Alison's gift makes her a conduit between the dead and the living, he begs her to pass on a message from the other side.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels and a lifetime of photos from her years as a singing waitress, let go of the past and remind her that she's not dead yet.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan insists that they purchase an expensive over the top burglar alarm after Brigitte thinks her van has been stolen, but it fails to serve its purpose. Also, Nick's career takes an unexpected turn.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When Nick suggests his parents take out a life insurance policy to further his chances of romance, Michael begins to worry about their life expectancy.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick wants a job, Janey wants new jeans, Michael needs dating tips, and Ben and Susan want peace and quiet.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan finds Viagra in her son's trousers and confiscates it, Janey tries to get Marilyn Manson tickets, Susan lets her yoga instructor give lessons at her house and Ben is put on a diet.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When Susan tries to help out a `chronically unemployable' Nick, her plan turns out to be disastrous, Janey brings a different boy home every day, and Ben has problems with his assistant.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan face the surprise prospect of parenthood for the fourth time - a situation which is neither wholly desirable nor planned.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The nation's favourite vet returns with more cutting-edge care. In this rescue special, we meet abandoned seven-week-old kitten Pretzel and look back at Wobble the whippet.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats tea-drinking Bedlington terrier Muffin, who has a fractured leg due to her limb deformity, and fox red Labrador Jethro, whose arthritic elbows are stopping him from walking properly.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel gives Maine coon cat Lexie a bilateral total hip replacement, while one-year-old bouncy Border terrier Charlie is in pain and is struggling to walk, and dwarf rabbit Locky has broken his leg.

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews treat a boy hit by a car outside school, a pensioner with a huge lump on his head and a woman with suspected sepsis.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Dr Fox is in the visitors' room for a change when his fiancee Sally has an operation to remove her wisdom teeth. Another patient has surgery to have a double hernia repaired.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre

programme.title: Deadly Waters With Captain Lee
programme.desc: When a Bahamas-bound charter boat is discovered abandoned 140 miles off course, investigators wade through blood evidence and accounts of a possible pirate attack in their search for survivors and answers.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting; the Death Cleaners help her face her fear of discussing "the end."

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics help a man suffering from anxiety who discovered he might have been exposed to HIV. Meanwhile, crews attend to a man found unconscious in the stairwell of a block of flats.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along The US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border, in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she tries just about every sample in a Tequila showroom and is left in no doubt that the city deserves its reputation as a party town. She also discovers an unexpected invasion of migrants: American retirees who have settled south of the border to enjoy the good life and an ocean view at an affordable price. Over on the US side of the border, Sue spends a day with Mark Lamb, sheriff of Pinal County.

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along The US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Sue visits the Mexican city of Juarez, essentially one half of a super-city adjoining El Paso, Texas. Here she meets people whose lives traverse the border, including Gabriela, who is bringing up her kids alone in El Paso because her husband, Adrian, is not legal in America and is stuck in Juarez. Every weekend she takes the children to the border, so they can cross into Mexico and be with their dad. But Gabriela is not allowed to cross the border until her own immigration papers are finalised.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will and Gus buy a Porsche 923 with a cracked interior, bad paintwork and ripped seats. This driving legend is well past its best, but can they still turn a profit?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will and Gus look to make some quick cash as they fix and wrap a Toyota MR2 MK2. But is there money to be made in this low-spec car?

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will benefit from an owner's decision that his broken vehicle is beyond repair, and secure a Mercedes 190 investment that they hope to restore.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will find an old Alfa 155 in a barn, which is entirely original. The price seems good too, but on taking a second look, the undercarriage of the vehicle appears to be in a sorry state.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The festive season falls upon the inmates of HMP Slade and finds them planning an escape. The doctor, however, likes to keep the place empty.

programme.title: Porridge

programme.desc: Simon Reeve visits the tropical Indian Ocean islands of Madagascar, Mauritius and the Seychelles. programme.desc: By pushing themselves and their bodies to the limit, mammals have found remarkable ways to survive in the hottest places on earth. In baking deserts, harsh dry spiny forests, barren desolate salt pans and even bush fires, mammals beat the heat.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James reveals the cars that turned post-war Germany and Japan into motoring powerhouses at the expense of Britain and the US. On his travels he encounters classic E-Types, Mustangs and the German and Japanese upstarts. He also has an unfortunate encounter with an Austin Allegro - the car that helped destroy the British car industry.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Augusta to Darwin: The Ghan
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures down under to experience one of the world's greatest rail journeys aboard the legendary Ghan Railway. His 1913 Bradshaw's Guide tells him `a transcontinental railway is being constructed' and he traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to the port of Darwin on the north coast.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores New South Wales, steered by his 1913 Bradshaw's Guide. He strikes west into the Blue Mountains and travels 570 miles into the interior of the vast Australian continent to reach the mining city of Broken Hill. In the waterside metropolis of Sydney, the NSW capital, Michael investigates the origins of the city as a penal colony, visiting the Hyde Park Barracks, where many of the first convicts were housed.

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Adelaide to Perth: The Indian Pacific
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a rail adventure aboard the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway, from the southern port city of Adelaide, South Australia, 1,700 miles deep into the desert, to the gold rush town of Kalgoorlie, and finishes in Perth, Western Australia. First, Michael explores McLaren Vale, where an enterprising early 19th century Briton with a murky past began to grow vines.

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins his journey in the biggest city in the country, Yangon, and drives north into Burma's Buddhist heartlands and the stunning ancient capital of Bagan, a sight that rivals the great wonders of the world.

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon journeys up the vast Irrawaddy river to the old royal capital of Mandalay, home to an exotic market for precious jade. In Burma's mountainous highlands, he experiences Burma's vibrant ethnic heritage at an extraordinary and explosive fire balloon festival, then visits an elephant sanctuary and finds out how a growing tourism industry is helping them survive after working for decades in the logging industry.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to one of the most spectacular countries in the world, Colombia, to explore the country's precarious future.

programme.title: Gorillas Revisited with Sir David Attenborough
programme.desc: Ever since they were discovered over a century ago, gorillas have been threatened by loss of habitat, poaching, disease and political instability, But despite all odds their numbers have increased. Sir David tells the extraordinary tale of how conservationists like Dian Fossey have battled to save the mountain gorilla from the brink of extinction.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: On the Jurassic coast of Britain a remarkable 200-million-year-old fossil is discovered: the bones of an ichthyosaur, a giant sea dragon. David Attenborough joins the hunt to bring this ancient creature's story to life. Using state-of-the-art imaging technology and CGI, the team reconstruct the skeleton and create the most detailed animation of an ichthyosaur ever made.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie visits a former Beatle's home in Henley, uncovers horror history in Marlow and struggles to find a mooring in Maidenhead on the River Thames.

programme.desc: Victor and Margaret go on a trip to Whipsnade Zoo, but their outing turns into a nightmare when they unexpectedly become imprisoned like the animals.

programme.desc: Victor and Margaret battle to rid their home of an evil Moroccan curse. Meanwhile, Victor's neighbours put their house up for sale, but there are very few takers.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
